  • multi-agent
  • AI-workforce
  • CAMEL-AI
  • MCP
  • automation

Oh My OpenCode: Claude Code on Steroids - The Ultimate AI Agent Harness

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development tools, OpenCode has emerged as a formidable open-source alternative to commercial AI coding assistants. But if you’re a hacker who loves customization and maximum control, Oh My OpenCode is the plugin that transforms OpenCode into something extraordinary.

Created by developer code-yeongyu after spending $24,000 worth of tokens on personal development with various AI tools, Oh My OpenCode represents the distillation of countless hours of testing, configuration, and real-world usage. It’s described by its author as “OpenCode on steroids” — and for good reason.

“If Claude Code does in 7 days what a human does in 3 months, Sisyphus does it in 1 hour. It just works until the task is done. It is a disciplined agent.” — B, Quant Researcher

What is Oh My OpenCode?

Section titled “What is Oh My OpenCode?”

Oh My OpenCode is a comprehensive plugin for OpenCode that adds:

  • Async Subagents — Run multiple AI agents in parallel, just like Claude Code
  • Curated Agents — Pre-configured specialist agents with optimal models
  • Crafted Tools — LSP and AST grep tools for powerful code manipulation
  • Curated MCPs — Built-in Model Context Protocol servers for extended capabilities
  • Claude Code Compatibility — Full compatibility layer for Claude Code configs

The plugin is battle-tested in production environments and has already helped developers accomplish remarkable feats:

“Knocked out 8000 eslint warnings with Oh My Opencode, just in a day” — Jacob Ferrari

“I converted a 45k line tauri app into a SaaS web app overnight using Ohmyopencode and ralph loop.” — James Hargis

Meet Sisyphus: The Default Agent

Section titled “Meet Sisyphus: The Default Agent”

At the heart of Oh My OpenCode is Sisyphus, the default orchestrator agent named after the Greek mythological figure condemned to roll a boulder up a hill for eternity.

“In greek mythology, Sisyphus was condemned to roll a boulder up a hill for eternity as punishment for deceiving the gods. LLM Agents haven’t really done anything wrong, yet they too roll their ‘stones’—their thoughts—every single day. My life is no different. Looking back, we are not so different from these agents.”

Sisyphus’s Toolkit

Section titled “Sisyphus’s Toolkit”

Sisyphus comes equipped with a powerful set of tools and teammates:

Teammates (Curated Agents):

  • Oracle (GPT 5.2 Medium) — Design and debugging
  • Frontend UI/UX Engineer (Gemini 3 Pro) — Frontend development
  • Librarian (Claude Sonnet 4.5) — Official docs and codebase exploration
  • Explore (Grok Code) — Fast codebase exploration with contextual grep

Built-in Tools:

  • Full LSP/AstGrep support for decisive refactoring
  • Todo Continuation Enforcer — Forces agents to finish tasks
  • Comment Checker — Prevents excessive AI-generated comments
  • Claude Code Compatibility — Commands, agents, skills, MCPs, and hooks
  • Curated MCPs: Exa (web search), Context7 (official docs), Grep.app (GitHub search)
  • Async agents with background execution
  • Session management and recovery

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

1. Multi-Agent Orchestration

Section titled “1. Multi-Agent Orchestration”

Oh My OpenCode enables sophisticated workflows where different AI models work in parallel:

2. LSP and AST Tools

Section titled “2. LSP and AST Tools”

Unlike other AI harnesses that limit agent access to code analysis, Oh My OpenCode gives agents full access to:

  • lsp_hover — Type info and documentation
  • lsp_goto_definition — Jump to symbol definitions
  • lsp_find_references — Find all usages across workspace
  • lsp_document_symbols — Get file symbol outline
  • lsp_workspace_symbols — Search symbols by name
  • lsp_diagnostics — Get errors before building
  • lsp_rename — Rename symbols across workspace
  • ast_grep_search — AST-aware code pattern search
  • ast_grep_replace — AST-aware code replacement

3. Ralph Loop

Section titled “3. Ralph Loop”

Inspired by Anthropic’s Ralph Wiggum plugin, the Ralph Loop is a self-referential development loop that runs until task completion:

The loop detects <promise>DONE</promise> to know when complete and auto-continues if the agent stops prematurely. It supports all programming languages and can be configured with custom iteration limits.

4. Claude Code Compatibility

Section titled “4. Claude Code Compatibility”

Oh My OpenCode provides a comprehensive compatibility layer for Claude Code:

  • Hooks Integration — PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop
  • Config Loaders — Commands, Skills, Agents, MCP configs from standard locations
  • Data Storage — Todo management and transcripts in Claude Code format
  • Compatibility Toggles — Fine-grained control over which features to enable

5. Context Management

Section titled “5. Context Management”

Intelligent context management features:

  • Context Window Monitor — Alerts at 70%+ usage
  • Preemptive Compaction — Compacts session at 85% usage (enabled by default)
  • Anthropic Auto Compact — Automatic summarization at token limits
  • Tool Output Truncator — Prevents one search from eating your entire context

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Quick Install (Interactive)

Section titled “Quick Install (Interactive)”

Follow the prompts to configure your Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini subscriptions.

Agent-Assisted Install

Section titled “Agent-Assisted Install”

Paste this into a fresh OpenCode session:

The agent will guide you through the entire setup process.

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

Configuration is stored in JSON/JSONC format:

User config: ~/.config/opencode/oh-my-opencode.json Project config: .opencode/oh-my-opencode.json

Example Configuration

Section titled “Example Configuration”

Usage Modes

Section titled “Usage Modes”

Keywords for Specialized Modes

Section titled “Keywords for Specialized Modes”

Include these keywords in your prompts to activate specialized modes:

  • ultrawork / ulw — Maximum performance with parallel agent orchestration
  • search / find — Maximized search effort with parallel explore and librarian agents
  • analyze / investigate — Deep analysis mode with multi-phase expert consultation

Calling Specialized Agents

Section titled “Calling Specialized Agents”

Comparison with Claude Code

Section titled “Comparison with Claude Code”
FeatureClaude CodeOh My OpenCode
Multi-model supportClaude onlyClaude, GPT, Gemini, Grok
Async agentsYesYes
LSP toolsAnalysis onlyFull refactoring
HooksYesYes (compatible)
Custom agentsSkills systemCurated + custom
ExtensibilityLimitedHighly extensible
Open sourceNoYes

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

1. Start Simple

Section titled “1. Start Simple”

If the configuration seems overwhelming, just remember: include the word ultrawork in your prompt. The agent will figure out the rest.

2. Use Background Agents for Parallel Work

Section titled “2. Use Background Agents for Parallel Work”

Let different models work on different parts simultaneously:

3. Leverage Context Management

Section titled “3. Leverage Context Management”

Oh My OpenCode aggressively uses multiple agents to lighten context load. Your agent becomes the dev team lead, and you’re the AI manager.

4. Customize for Your Workflow

Section titled “4. Customize for Your Workflow”

You can disable features you don’t need:

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Issue: Bunx fails on Ubuntu/Debian

Section titled “Issue: Bunx fails on Ubuntu/Debian”

If you installed Bun via Snap, bunx will fail due to sandboxing. Use npx instead or reinstall Bun via the official installer.

Issue: OpenCode version compatibility

Section titled “Issue: OpenCode version compatibility”

Ensure OpenCode version is 1.0.150 or higher:

Issue: Authentication errors

Section titled “Issue: Authentication errors”

For Google Gemini, consider using the external opencode-antigravity-auth plugin for multi-account load balancing.

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: Do I need all AI subscriptions?

A: No! You can use Oh My OpenCode with just one provider. The plugin supports Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini, but you pick what you have.

Q: Is this suitable for beginners?

A: The plugin is powerful but can be complex. The author recommends including ultrawork in your prompts — the agent will handle optimization automatically.

Q: How does this compare to AmpCode?

A: Oh My OpenCode is heavily influenced by AmpCode and Claude Code, with features ported and often improved. It’s designed for the open-source OpenCode platform.

Q: Can I use this with existing Claude Code configs?

A: Yes! The Claude Code compatibility layer means your existing configurations, hooks, and skills should work out of the box.

Community and Resources

Section titled “Community and Resources”
  • GitHub Repository: code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode
  • OpenCode Documentation: opencode.ai/docs
  • Video Review: Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane
  • Medium Article: A Deep Dive into Oh My OpenCode
  • Reddit Community: r/opencodeCLI

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Oh My OpenCode represents the culmination of extensive real-world testing and refinement. It transforms OpenCode from a capable AI coding assistant into a sophisticated multi-agent orchestration platform.

Whether you’re a seasoned developer looking to maximize productivity or an AI enthusiast exploring the boundaries of what’s possible, Oh My OpenCode offers a powerful, extensible, and thoroughly tested solution.

As the author puts it:

“Stop agonizing over agent harness choices. I’ll do the research, borrow from the best, and ship updates here.”

Ready to supercharge your OpenCode experience? Install Oh My OpenCode and let Sisyphus keep that boulder rolling.

Sources:

  • Oh My OpenCode GitHub Repository
  • OpenCode Official Site
  • YouTube: Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane
  • Medium: A Deep Dive into Oh My OpenCode
Tags:
  • AI
  • Claude Code
  • OpenCode
  • AI Agents
  • Developer Tools
  • Multi-Agent
  • LSP
  • MCP

pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants

Section titled “pg-aiguide: AI-Optimized PostgreSQL Expertise for Coding Assistants”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development tools, one challenge has persisted: AI coding assistants often generate database code that is outdated, missing critical constraints, unaware of modern PostgreSQL features, and inconsistent with real-world best practices.

Enter pg-aiguide – an innovative MCP server and Claude plugin developed by Timescale (now Tiger Data) that provides AI-optimized PostgreSQL expertise to coding assistants.

What is pg-aiguide?

Section titled “What is pg-aiguide?”

pg-aiguide is an open-source tool (Apache-2.0 licensed) that helps AI coding tools write dramatically better PostgreSQL code. It provides three core capabilities:

  1. Semantic Search across the official PostgreSQL manual (version-aware)
  2. AI-Optimized Skills – curated, opinionated Postgres best practices used automatically by AI agents
  3. Extension Ecosystem Docs, starting with TimescaleDB, with more extensions coming soon

The Problem pg-aiguide Solves

Section titled “The Problem pg-aiguide Solves”

AI coding tools frequently generate PostgreSQL code that suffers from several issues:

  • Outdated syntax – not keeping up with modern PostgreSQL versions
  • Missing constraints and indexes – leading to poor data integrity and performance
  • Unaware of modern PG features – missing out on capabilities introduced in recent versions
  • Inconsistent with best practices – not following proven patterns from experienced developers

Real-World Impact

Section titled “Real-World Impact”

The pg-aiguide team conducted an impressive comparison test. When asked to create an e-commerce database schema:

  • 4× more constraints compared to AI without pg-aiguide
  • 55% more indexes (including partial and expression indexes)
  • PG17-recommended patterns – using the latest PostgreSQL features
  • Modern features like GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY and NULLS NOT DISTINCT
  • Cleaner naming & documentation – more maintainable code

Installation & Setup

Section titled “Installation & Setup”

pg-aiguide can be used in two ways:

As a Public MCP Server

Section titled “As a Public MCP Server”

Available at https://mcp.tigerdata.com/docs, it can be configured for any AI coding agent:

As a Claude Code Plugin

Section titled “As a Claude Code Plugin”

For Claude Code users, installation is straightforward:

Supported AI Tools

Section titled “Supported AI Tools”

pg-aiguide supports a wide range of AI coding environments:

  • Claude Code – Native plugin support
  • Cursor – One-click install available
  • Gemini CLI – MCP server integration
  • VS Code / VS Code Insiders – Command-line installation
  • Windsurf – Configuration-based setup
  • Codex – MCP server integration

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Semantic Documentation Search

Section titled “Semantic Documentation Search”

The semantic_search_postgres_docs function performs semantic search over the official PostgreSQL manual, with results scoped to specific Postgres versions. This means AI agents get accurate, version-relevant information.

Extension Ecosystem Support

Section titled “Extension Ecosystem Support”

Through semantic_search_tiger_docs, pg-aiguide searches Tiger Data’s documentation corpus, including TimescaleDB and future ecosystem extensions.

Curated Best Practices

Section titled “Curated Best Practices”

The view_skill function exposes opinionated PostgreSQL best-practice skills covering:

  • Schema design patterns
  • Indexing strategies
  • Data type selection
  • Data integrity and constraints
  • Naming conventions
  • Performance tuning
  • Modern PostgreSQL features

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

Simple Schema Design

Section titled “Simple Schema Design”

Prompt: “Create a Postgres table schema for storing usernames and unique email addresses.”

pg-aiguide helps generate properly constrained schemas with appropriate indexes and modern PostgreSQL types.

Complex IoT Schema

Section titled “Complex IoT Schema”

For more complex scenarios, like an IoT device monitoring system:

“Generate a Postgres schema for an IoT device company. Devices collect environmental data including temperature, humidity, pressure, and other measurements. Each device has a unique ID and human-readable name. Analysis includes finding outliers, anomalies, and device-specific ad-hoc analysis.”

pg-aiguide helps design schemas optimized for both recent data analysis and historical reporting.

Technology Stack

Section titled “Technology Stack”
  • Python (66.5%) – Core server logic
  • TypeScript (23.1%) – Plugin integrations
  • JavaScript (9.3%) – Web interfaces

Future Plans

Section titled “Future Plans”

The project has an ambitious roadmap:

  • pgvector support for vector similarity search
  • PostGIS for geospatial data
  • Community contributions for additional extensions

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

pg-aiguide welcomes contributions in several areas:

  • New Postgres best-practice skills
  • Additional documentation corpora
  • Search quality improvements
  • Bug reports and feature ideas

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

pg-aiguide represents a significant step forward in AI-assisted database development. By providing AI agents with deep, versioned PostgreSQL knowledge and proven patterns, it bridges the gap between AI-generated code and production-quality database schemas.

Whether you’re a developer using AI coding assistants or a team building AI-powered development tools, pg-aiguide offers a practical solution to the persistent problem of suboptimal AI-generated database code.

Links

Section titled “Links”
  • GitHub: timescale/pg-aiguide
  • MCP Server: https://mcp.tigerdata.com/docs
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • Author: Timescale (Tiger Data)

Published: December 31, 2025

Tags:
  • postgresql
  • ai
  • mcp
  • claude-code
  • database
  • developer-tools
  • timescale

Continuous Claude: The Ultimate Context Management System for Claude Code

Dec 25, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Continuous Claude: The Ultimate Context Management System for Claude Code

Section titled “Continuous Claude: The Ultimate Context Management System for Claude Code”

When working with Claude Code on complex projects, you’ve likely encountered the frustrating “context degradation” problem. After several compaction cycles, you’re essentially working with a summary of a summary of a summary. Signal degrades into noise, and Claude starts hallucinating context.

Continuous Claude solves this fundamental problem through a revolutionary approach: clear, don’t compact.

The Core Problem: Context Compaction Decay

Section titled “The Core Problem: Context Compaction Decay”

Traditional approaches try to optimize compaction algorithms. Continuous Claude takes a fundamentally different path: preserve state externally, clear context completely, resume with full signal.

The Solution: Ledgers and Handoffs

Section titled “The Solution: Ledgers and Handoffs”

Why This Works

Section titled “Why This Works”
  • Ledgers are lossless - You control exactly what’s saved
  • Fresh context = full signal - No degraded summaries
  • Agents spawn clean - Each agent gets isolated context windows

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

1. Continuity System

Section titled “1. Continuity System”

Within-Session (Ledger)

Before running /clear:

Creates/updates CONTINUITY_CLAUDE-<session>.md with:

  • Goal and constraints
  • What’s done, what’s next
  • Key decisions
  • Working files

Between-Sessions (Handoff)

When done for the day:

Creates detailed handoff with:

  • Complete context
  • Recent changes with file:line references
  • Learnings and patterns
  • Next steps

2. Hook System Architecture

Section titled “2. Hook System Architecture”

Continuous Claude uses a sophisticated hook system that intercepts Claude Code lifecycle events:

EventWhenWhat It Does
SessionStartNew session, /clear, compactLoads ledger + latest handoff into context
PreCompactBefore context compactionCreates auto-handoff, blocks manual compact
UserPromptSubmitBefore processing user messageShows skill suggestions, context warnings
PostToolUseAfter Edit/Write/BashTracks modified files for auto-summary
SubagentStopAgent finishesLogs agent completion
SessionEndSession closesCleanup temp files

3. MCP Code Execution Without Context Pollution

Section titled “3. MCP Code Execution Without Context Pollution”

Tools are executed via scripts, not loaded into context. This saves significant tokens:

4. Agent Orchestration

Section titled “4. Agent Orchestration”

For complex implementations, the implement_plan orchestrator spawns task agents:

Each task agent:

  1. Reads previous handoff
  2. Does its work with TDD
  3. Creates handoff for next agent
  4. Returns summary to orchestrator

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

Option 1: Use in a Single Project

Section titled “Option 1: Use in a Single Project”

Option 2: Install Globally

Section titled “Option 2: Install Globally”

Option 3: Initialize a New Project

Section titled “Option 3: Initialize a New Project”

Natural Language Triggers

Section titled “Natural Language Triggers”

Continuous Claude responds to natural language. Say certain phrases and Claude activates the right skill or spawns an agent.

Session Management

Section titled “Session Management”
Say ThisWhat Happens
”save state”, “update ledger”, “before clear”Updates continuity ledger
”done for today”, “wrap up”, “create handoff”Creates detailed handoff doc
”resume work”, “continue from handoff”Loads handoff, continues

Planning & Implementation

Section titled “Planning & Implementation”
Say ThisWhat Happens
”create plan”, “design”, “architect”Spawns plan-agent
”validate plan”, “before implementing”Spawns validate-agent
”implement plan”, “execute plan”Spawns implement_plan

Code Quality

Section titled “Code Quality”
Say ThisWhat Happens
”implement”, “add feature”, “fix bug”TDD workflow activates
”lint”, “code quality”, “auto-fix”Runs qlty-check
”commit”, “push”, “save changes”Runs commit skill

StatusLine: Real-Time Context Indicator

Section titled “StatusLine: Real-Time Context Indicator”

The colored status bar shows context usage:

Color coding:

  • 🟢 Green (< 60%): Normal
  • 🟡 Yellow (60-79%): Warning - consider handoff soon
  • 🔴 Red (≥ 80%): Critical - create handoff now

Skills vs Agents

Section titled “Skills vs Agents”

Skills run in current context - quick, focused, minimal token overhead.

Agents spawn with fresh context - use for complex tasks that would degrade in compacted context.

When to Use Agents

Section titled “When to Use Agents”
  • Brownfield exploration → rp-explorer
  • Multi-step research → research-agent
  • Complex debugging → debug-agent
  • Implementation with handoffs → implement_plan

Optional Integrations

Section titled “Optional Integrations”

Braintrust Session Tracing

Section titled “Braintrust Session Tracing”

Track every session for learning from past work:

  1. Session traces - Every turn, tool call, and LLM response logged
  2. Automatic learnings - Extracts “What Worked/Failed/Patterns” at session end
  3. Artifact Index integration - Handoffs linked to trace IDs

Compound Learnings

Section titled “Compound Learnings”

Transform session learnings into permanent rules:

Directory Structure

Section titled “Directory Structure”

Comparison: Traditional vs Continuous Claude

Section titled “Comparison: Traditional vs Continuous Claude”
AspectTraditional ApproachContinuous Claude
Context DegradationCumulative lossZero loss
Session ContinuityManual notesAutomated ledgers
Agent ContextInherited degradedFresh isolated
Learning LoopNoneCompound learnings
Token EfficiencyTools loaded in contextScripts executed externally

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Update ledger before /clear - Always save state first
  2. Create handoffs at stopping points - Don’t wait until context is critical
  3. Use agents for complex tasks - They get clean context
  4. Leverage compound learnings - Turn patterns into rules
  5. Watch the StatusLine - It shows when to create handoffs

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Continuous Claude represents a paradigm shift in how we approach context management for AI coding assistants. By embracing “clear, don’t compact” and building robust state preservation through ledgers and handoffs, it enables:

  • Lossless context preservation across sessions
  • Token-efficient MCP execution without context pollution
  • Intelligent agent orchestration with isolated context windows
  • Continuous learning that improves over time

For teams working on complex, long-running projects with Claude Code, Continuous Claude is an essential addition to your development toolkit.

References

Section titled “References”
  • Continuous Claude GitHub Repository
  • Anthropic MCP Documentation
  • Braintrust Claude Plugin
Tags:
  • Claude Code
  • Context Management
  • MCP

MCP Defender: Docker's New AI Agent Security Shield

Dec 25, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Docker Acquires MCP Defender: The Security Layer AI Agents Have Been Missing

As AI agents become increasingly autonomous in our development workflows, the security landscape has fundamentally shifted. Enter MCP Defender—a desktop app that Docker recently acquired to protect AI applications like Cursor, Claude Desktop, VS Code, and Windsurf from a growing array of attacks.

1. What is MCP Defender?

Section titled “1. What is MCP Defender?”

MCP Defender is a desktop application that acts as a security proxy for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Here’s how it works:

  • 🛡️ Automatic Protection: All MCP tool call requests and responses from AI apps are automatically proxied through MCP Defender
  • 🔎 Signature-Based Detection: Intercepted data is checked against a set of security signatures
  • 🔐 User-Controlled Blocking: When harmful content is detected, MCP Defender alerts you and asks whether to allow or block the tool call

Supported AI Apps:

  • Cursor
  • Claude Desktop
  • Visual Studio Code
  • Windsurf

2. Why Docker Acquired MCP Defender

Section titled “2. Why Docker Acquired MCP Defender”

On September 5, 2025, Docker announced the acquisition of MCP Defender, marking a strategic move into AI agent security. The rationale is clear:

“AI agents are getting smarter—and more autonomous. That means new risks, new attack surfaces, and a need for runtime security that moves as fast as dev teams do.”

The Security Gap

Section titled “The Security Gap”

Traditional security tools weren’t designed for AI agents. When an AI agent has the ability to:

  • Execute code
  • Access file systems
  • Make API calls
  • Interact with external services

…the attack surface expands dramatically. MCP Defender fills this critical gap by providing:

CapabilityDescription
Runtime MonitoringContinuous observation of AI agent behavior
Policy EnforcementCustomizable security rules for AI operations
Real-time Threat DetectionIdentifies malicious patterns as they occur
Multi-Client ProtectionSecures multiple AI apps simultaneously

3. Getting Started

Section titled “3. Getting Started”

Quick Install (Mac)

Section titled “Quick Install (Mac)”

Download the latest release directly:

  • Download MCP Defender for Mac

From Source

Section titled “From Source”

Once running, MCP Defender automatically intercepts MCP traffic from supported AI apps—no additional configuration needed.

4. How It Protects You

Section titled “4. How It Protects You”

MCP Defender guards against several attack vectors:

🔴 Prompt Injection Attacks

Section titled “🔴 Prompt Injection Attacks”

Malicious instructions embedded in data that trick AI agents into executing harmful commands.

🔴 Data Exfiltration

Section titled “🔴 Data Exfiltration”

Prevents unauthorized data extraction through AI tool calls.

🔴 Unauthorized Tool Execution

Section titled “🔴 Unauthorized Tool Execution”

Blocks attempts to execute tools or commands beyond the intended scope.

🔴 Supply Chain Attacks

Section titled “🔴 Supply Chain Attacks”

Detects compromised MCP servers or malicious tool definitions.

5. Docker’s Security Vision

Section titled “5. Docker’s Security Vision”

The acquisition fits into Docker’s broader “secure-by-default AI infrastructure” vision:

  • Docker Scout: Container image vulnerability scanning
  • Docker Hardened Images: Pre-secured base images
  • Docker MCP Gateway: Secure MCP server hosting
  • MCP Defender: Runtime AI agent protection

Together, these tools aim to defend the entire AI software supply chain while reducing the security burden on development teams.

6. Real-World Demo

Section titled “6. Real-World Demo”

MCP Defender has demonstrated its value against real attack scenarios:

Attack TypeHow MCP Defender Responds
WhatsApp Data ExfiltrationBlocks unauthorized access to messaging data
File System ManipulationAlerts when AI attempts to access sensitive directories
Code Injection via Tool CallsIntercepts and flags malicious payloads

The app shows a clear alert dialog when threats are detected, giving you full control over whether to allow or block the action.

7. Final Thoughts

Section titled “7. Final Thoughts”

AI agents are transforming how we build software, but with great power comes great responsibility—and greater attack surfaces. MCP Defender represents a crucial evolution in AI security:

  • For Individual Developers: Peace of mind when using AI coding assistants
  • For Enterprise Teams: Compliance-ready AI agent governance
  • For the Ecosystem: A foundation for secure agentic AI infrastructure

With Docker’s backing, MCP Defender is positioned to become the standard for AI agent security. If you’re using Cursor, Claude Desktop, VS Code, or Windsurf, installing MCP Defender is now a no-brainer.

References

Section titled “References”
  • Docker Acquires MCP Defender
  • MCP Defender GitHub Repository
  • MCP Defender Official Site
  • Docker on X - Acquisition Announcement
Tags:
  • AI Agent
  • MCP
  • Docker
  • Security
  • Claude
  • Cursor

Vibe Sparking

MCPCAN: Centralized MCP Service Management Platform

Dec 24, 2025
Vibe Sparking

MCPCAN: Centralized MCP Service Management Platform

Section titled “MCPCAN: Centralized MCP Service Management Platform”

As the Model Context Protocol (MCP) ecosystem continues to expand, managing multiple MCP services across development and production environments has become increasingly complex. MCPCAN emerges as a comprehensive solution - a centralized management platform that deploys MCP services using containerized approaches while providing robust security, monitoring, and protocol conversion capabilities.

What is MCPCAN?

Section titled “What is MCPCAN?”

MCPCAN is an open-source, Kubernetes-native platform designed for DevOps teams to manage the full lifecycle of MCP services. It addresses critical challenges in MCP deployment:

  • Configuration Conflicts: Container isolation eliminates system-level conflicts
  • Security Vulnerabilities: Built-in token verification and permission management
  • Deployment Complexity: One-click deployment with Helm charts
  • Protocol Fragmentation: Seamless conversion between SSE, STDIO, and STREAMABLEHTTP

Core Features

Section titled “Core Features”

1. Unified Management Dashboard

Section titled “1. Unified Management Dashboard”

The Vue.js-powered web interface provides centralized control over all MCP service instances:

  • Visual service configuration management
  • Real-time status monitoring
  • Batch operations support
  • Service lifecycle control (start/stop/restart)

2. Multi-Protocol Support

Section titled “2. Multi-Protocol Support”

MCPCAN supports three primary MCP access protocols:

ProtocolUse CaseCharacteristics
SSEReal-time streamingServer-Sent Events for continuous updates
STDIOLocal integrationStandard input/output for CLI tools
STREAMABLEHTTPWeb applicationsHTTP-based streaming with broad compatibility

The platform handles protocol conversion automatically, allowing services written for one protocol to be accessed via another.

3. Security Authentication System

Section titled “3. Security Authentication System”

Security is built into MCPCAN’s core architecture:

  • Token Verification: Every MCP service request is validated
  • Identity Authentication: User-level access control
  • Permission Management: Role-based service access
  • Audit Logging: Complete request tracking

4. Container-Based Deployment

Section titled “4. Container-Based Deployment”

Each MCP service runs in its own isolated container:

Benefits include:

  • Resource isolation and limits
  • Independent scaling
  • Easy rollback and versioning
  • Consistent environments

5. Real-Time Monitoring

Section titled “5. Real-Time Monitoring”

Comprehensive observability features:

  • Service health dashboards
  • Performance metrics collection
  • Resource utilization tracking
  • Alert configuration

Architecture Overview

Section titled “Architecture Overview”

MCPCAN follows a microservices architecture built on Kubernetes:

Technology Stack

Section titled “Technology Stack”

Frontend:

  • Vue.js 3.5+ with Composition API
  • TypeScript for type safety
  • Pinia for state management
  • Element Plus UI components
  • Vite build tooling

Backend:

  • Go 1.24.2+ for high performance
  • Gin web framework
  • gRPC for internal communication
  • MySQL for persistent storage
  • Redis for caching and sessions

Installation Guide

Section titled “Installation Guide”

Quick Installation (Recommended)

Section titled “Quick Installation (Recommended)”

For rapid deployment with automated setup:

This automatically installs:

  • k3s (lightweight Kubernetes)
  • ingress-nginx
  • Helm 3
  • MCPCAN components

Custom Installation

Section titled “Custom Installation”

For existing Kubernetes clusters or custom configurations:

Configuration Options

Section titled “Configuration Options”

Key configuration parameters:

ParameterDescriptionDefault
domainPlatform access domainlocalhost
tls.enabledEnable HTTPSfalse
mysql.storageSizeDatabase storage10Gi
redis.enabledEnable Redis cachingtrue

Usage Scenarios

Section titled “Usage Scenarios”

Scenario 1: Enterprise MCP Gateway

Section titled “Scenario 1: Enterprise MCP Gateway”

Deploy MCPCAN as a centralized gateway for all AI assistant tools:

  1. Register MCP services through the marketplace
  2. Configure access tokens for each team
  3. Monitor usage and performance
  4. Scale services based on demand

Scenario 2: Development Team Sandbox

Section titled “Scenario 2: Development Team Sandbox”

Provide isolated MCP environments for developers:

  • Each developer gets dedicated service instances
  • Shared service pool for common tools
  • Development/staging/production separation

Scenario 3: Multi-Tenant Platform

Section titled “Scenario 3: Multi-Tenant Platform”

Operate MCP services for multiple clients:

  • Tenant isolation through namespaces
  • Per-tenant resource quotas
  • Separate billing and monitoring

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

Security Hardening

Section titled “Security Hardening”
  1. Enable TLS: Always use HTTPS in production
  2. Rotate Tokens: Implement regular token rotation
  3. Network Policies: Restrict inter-service communication
  4. Audit Everything: Enable comprehensive logging

Performance Optimization

Section titled “Performance Optimization”
  1. Resource Limits: Set appropriate CPU/memory limits
  2. Horizontal Scaling: Use HPA for auto-scaling
  3. Caching: Leverage Redis for frequent requests
  4. Connection Pooling: Optimize database connections

Operational Excellence

Section titled “Operational Excellence”
  1. GitOps: Store configurations in Git
  2. Monitoring: Set up alerts for critical metrics
  3. Backup: Regular database backups
  4. Documentation: Maintain runbooks

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
FeatureMCPCANManual DockerCloud Functions
Container Isolation✅✅❌
Protocol Conversion✅❌❌
Centralized Auth✅❌Partial
Visual Dashboard✅❌Varies
Kubernetes Native✅❌❌
Open Source✅✅❌

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Common Issues

Section titled “Common Issues”

Service Won’t Start

Token Verification Fails

  • Verify token format matches service configuration
  • Check token expiration
  • Review audit logs for details

High Latency

  • Check resource utilization
  • Review network policies
  • Enable Redis caching

Community and Support

Section titled “Community and Support”
  • GitHub: Kymo-MCP/mcpcan
  • Discord: Community chat for discussions
  • Documentation: mcpcan.com
  • Demo: demo.mcpcan.com

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

MCPCAN represents a significant step forward in MCP service management, bringing enterprise-grade features to the MCP ecosystem. Whether you’re a solo developer managing a few MCP tools or an enterprise team operating dozens of services, MCPCAN provides the infrastructure needed for reliable, secure, and scalable MCP deployments.

The platform’s combination of container isolation, protocol flexibility, and built-in security makes it an excellent choice for teams looking to standardize their MCP operations while maintaining control over their infrastructure.

License: Apache License 2.0

Project Repository: https://github.com/Kymo-MCP/mcpcan

Tags:
  • MCP
  • MCPCAN
  • Kubernetes
  • Container Management
  • DevOps
  • Model Context Protocol

Claude Scientific Skills: 125+ Ready-to-Use AI-Powered Scientific Research Workflows

Dec 22, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Claude Scientific Skills: 125+ Ready-to-Use AI-Powered Scientific Research Workflows

Section titled “Claude Scientific Skills: 125+ Ready-to-Use AI-Powered Scientific Research Workflows”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-assisted research, Claude Scientific Skills emerges as a game-changing toolkit that transforms Claude into a powerful scientific research assistant. Created by K-Dense-AI, this comprehensive collection of 125+ specialized skills enables researchers to leverage AI across diverse scientific domains without writing complex prompts from scratch.

What is Claude Scientific Skills?

Section titled “What is Claude Scientific Skills?”

Claude Scientific Skills is an open-source collection of ready-to-use scientific skills designed specifically for Claude. Each skill is a carefully crafted prompt template that guides Claude to perform specific scientific tasks with precision and domain expertise.

“Transform your Claude assistant into a specialized scientific research tool with just a few clicks.”

Key Statistics

Section titled “Key Statistics”
  • 125+ Scientific Skills covering diverse research domains
  • 26+ Scientific Databases integrated for data access
  • 54+ Python Packages recommended for scientific computing
  • 15+ Scientific Integrations with industry platforms
  • 20+ Real-World Workflows documented with examples

Core Features & Highlights

Section titled “Core Features & Highlights”

1. Comprehensive Domain Coverage

Section titled “1. Comprehensive Domain Coverage”

Claude Scientific Skills spans multiple scientific disciplines:

Bioinformatics & Genomics:

  • Sequence analysis and alignment
  • Variant calling and annotation
  • Gene expression analysis
  • Pathway enrichment studies

Cheminformatics & Drug Discovery:

  • Molecular property prediction
  • ADMET analysis
  • Chemical similarity searching
  • Compound library design

Proteomics & Structural Biology:

  • Protein structure prediction
  • Binding site analysis
  • Post-translational modification detection
  • Protein-protein interaction networks

Clinical Research:

  • Patient cohort analysis
  • Biomarker discovery
  • Clinical trial design assistance
  • Survival analysis workflows

Medical Imaging:

  • Image preprocessing pipelines
  • Feature extraction workflows
  • Radiomics analysis
  • Multi-modal image fusion

Machine Learning & AI:

  • Model selection guidance
  • Hyperparameter optimization
  • Feature engineering strategies
  • Experiment tracking workflows

2. Integrated Scientific Databases

Section titled “2. Integrated Scientific Databases”

Access 26+ databases directly through skills:

CategoryDatabases
LiteratureOpenAlex, PubMed, Semantic Scholar
ChemicalChEMBL, PubChem, DrugBank
BiologicalUniProt, Ensembl, COSMIC
ClinicalClinicalTrials.gov, PharmGKB
ProteinPDB, AlphaFold DB
PathwayReactome, KEGG

3. Python Package Ecosystem

Section titled “3. Python Package Ecosystem”

The skills recommend and integrate with 54+ scientific Python packages:

4. Industry Platform Integrations

Section titled “4. Industry Platform Integrations”

Connect with 15+ scientific platforms:

  • Benchling - Lab notebook and molecular biology
  • DNAnexus - Genomics data analysis
  • LatchBio - Bioinformatics workflows
  • OMERO - Microscopy data management
  • Protocols.io - Method sharing
  • Weights & Biases - ML experiment tracking

Installation Options

Section titled “Installation Options”

Option 1: Claude Code Marketplace (Recommended)

Section titled “Option 1: Claude Code Marketplace (Recommended)”

The easiest way to install:

  1. Open Claude Code
  2. Navigate to the Marketplace
  3. Search for “K-Dense-AI Scientific Skills”
  4. Click Install

Option 2: Cursor IDE

Section titled “Option 2: Cursor IDE”

For Cursor users:

  1. Open Cursor Settings
  2. Go to AI > Skills
  3. Add the K-Dense-AI repository
  4. Enable desired skills

Option 3: Manual MCP Client Installation

Section titled “Option 3: Manual MCP Client Installation”

For any MCP-compatible client:

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

After installation, configure your preferred databases and integrations:

Real-World Workflow Examples

Section titled “Real-World Workflow Examples”

1. Drug Discovery Pipeline

Section titled “1. Drug Discovery Pipeline”

2. Clinical Variant Interpretation

Section titled “2. Clinical Variant Interpretation”

3. Single-Cell RNA Analysis

Section titled “3. Single-Cell RNA Analysis”

4. Protein Structure Analysis

Section titled “4. Protein Structure Analysis”

5. Literature Mining

Section titled “5. Literature Mining”

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

1. Skill Selection

Section titled “1. Skill Selection”
  • Choose the most specific skill for your task
  • Combine multiple skills for complex workflows
  • Start with exploratory skills before detailed analysis

2. Data Preparation

Section titled “2. Data Preparation”
  • Ensure data formats match skill requirements
  • Validate input data quality before analysis
  • Use recommended file formats (FASTA, PDB, CSV)

3. Result Validation

Section titled “3. Result Validation”
  • Cross-reference results with multiple databases
  • Verify computational predictions experimentally
  • Document assumptions and limitations

4. Workflow Documentation

Section titled “4. Workflow Documentation”
  • Save successful prompts for reuse
  • Track parameter choices and outcomes
  • Share workflows with team members

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureClaude Scientific SkillsChatGPT PluginsPerplexity Pro
Scientific Skills125+ specializedGeneral purposeGeneral purpose
Database Integration26+ databasesLimitedSearch-based
Python Integration54+ packagesCode interpreterLimited
Platform Integration15+ platformsFewNone
Custom WorkflowsYesLimitedNo
Open SourceYesNoNo
Offline CapableYesNoNo

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Section titled “Troubleshooting & FAQ”

Q: Skills not appearing in Claude?

Section titled “Q: Skills not appearing in Claude?”

A: Ensure the MCP server is properly configured and restart Claude Code.

Q: Database queries returning empty results?

Section titled “Q: Database queries returning empty results?”

A: Check API keys are configured correctly and database services are accessible.

Q: Python code not executing?

Section titled “Q: Python code not executing?”

A: Verify required packages are installed in your environment.

Q: How do I create custom skills?

Section titled “Q: How do I create custom skills?”

A: Follow the skill template in the repository’s templates/ directory.

Q: Can I use skills offline?

Section titled “Q: Can I use skills offline?”

A: Yes, most skills work offline. Database queries require internet access.

Q: How often are skills updated?

Section titled “Q: How often are skills updated?”

A: The repository is actively maintained with regular updates for new databases and methods.

Recent Updates

Section titled “Recent Updates”

The project is actively developed with regular additions:

  • New Quantum Computing Skills - Qiskit integration for quantum chemistry
  • Enhanced ML Workflows - PyTorch Lightning support
  • Clinical Trial Tools - ClinicalTrials.gov deep integration
  • Improved Documentation - 20+ detailed workflow examples

Community & Support

Section titled “Community & Support”
  • GitHub Repository: K-Dense-AI/claude-scientific-skills
  • Documentation: docs/examples.md
  • Issues: GitHub Issues
  • License: MIT (Free and Open Source)

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Claude Scientific Skills represents a significant advancement in AI-assisted scientific research. By providing 125+ specialized skills with deep integration into scientific databases, Python ecosystems, and industry platforms, it transforms Claude from a general-purpose assistant into a powerful research partner.

Whether you’re a bioinformatician analyzing genomic data, a chemist searching for drug candidates, a clinician interpreting variants, or a data scientist building ML pipelines, Claude Scientific Skills provides the domain expertise and workflow guidance you need.

The project’s commitment to open-source development, active maintenance, and comprehensive documentation makes it an essential toolkit for any researcher looking to leverage AI in their scientific workflows. Start with a single skill, explore the documented workflows, and discover how AI can accelerate your research.

  • Workflow Examples
  • MCP Protocol Documentation
  • Claude Code Setup Guide
  • OpenAlex API
  • ChEMBL Database
  • UniProt
  • ClinicalTrials.gov
